summarization.a: Heatmaps displaying λ correlation at various Reynolds numbers (Re) ranging from 40 to 2400, for injection directions tilted 90 degrees.
summarization.b: Heatmaps displaying λ correlation at various Reynolds numbers (Re) ranging from 40 to 2400, for opposite injection directions.
summarization.c: Heatmaps displaying thickness, after 10 and 600 cycles, for injection directions tilted 90 degrees.
summarization.d: Heatmaps comparing measured thickness at 10 and 600 cycles, for opposite injection directions.

a.question: Which Reynolds number show the highest uniformity?

c.question: From which Reynolds number on is there more chaotic flow?

d.question: Is the non-uniformity constant over time?
d.answer: d	No, the non-uniformity is not constant, for 10 and for 600 cycles the high spots are at completely different locations.

b.question: Is the deposition more dependent on the flow of one of the reactants?
b.answer: b	Yes, the deposition is more dependent on the flow coming from the black arrow, as there a thicker layer is measured.
